design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based combat with party management, exploration of overworld and dungeons, leveling through grinding, acquiring equipment and spells to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (HP/MP) during extended dungeon crawls; party composition choices affecting combat effectiveness
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lead a fellowship of diverse adventurers on an epic quest to save the world; experience each companion's personal story before uniting them
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Revolutionary chapter-based narrative structure where first four chapters follow different protagonists before converging in Chapter 5 with the Hero. Introduces AI-driven 'Tactics' system allowing party members to act autonomously with player-set strategies rather than direct control. This defies typical JRPG convention of full party control, foreshadowing systems like Final Fantasy XII's Gambits. The wagon system allows benching even the Hero, subverting series tradition.

difficulty.continues_system: Death returns player to last save; no permadeath
difficulty.checkpointing: Save points at Houses of Healing (churches/temples); separate from confession booths; no mid-dungeon checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Tactics system gives players strategic control over party behavior (damage focus, healing priority, MP conservation) without micromanagement. Players choose which characters to field via wagon system. Grinding available to over-level. No difficulty settings documented.

progression.upgrade_system: Traditional equipment (weapons, armor, shields), spells learned at specific character levels, stat growth through leveling, three key tiers (Thief, Magic, Ultimate) for door access
progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter progression gates content; character-specific abilities unlock at fixed levels; keys and travel doors reveal new areas; day/night cycles affect available encounters; ship and hot air balloon unlock late-game areas
replayability_hooks: Casino mini-games (slots, poker, monster betting) for special items
Small Medals scattered throughout world tradeable for unique rewards from secluded king
Searching drawers and jars for hidden items
Multiple party composition options via wagon system
AI tactics system encourages different strategic approaches

world_building_techniques: Chapter-based narrative focusing on different characters' motivations and backstories
Character-driven cutscenes establishing party members before Hero's arrival
Day/night cycle affecting NPC behavior and story moments
Environmental exploration revealing world details through towns and dungeons
Dialogue-heavy exposition explaining kingdom politics and monster threats
Convergence narrative where personal stories merge into overarching quest (Chapter 5 unification)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve narrative milestones for each chapter's protagonist; bosses are story-significant antagonists rather than abstract challenges. Final chapter brings all boss encounters into unified narrative arc.
notable_bosses: Psaro the Manslayer (implied final antagonist from narrative context)

faction_indicators: Monster sympathizers (Healie the Healslime befriending humans)
danger_signals: Missing children storyline (child abductions by forces unknown)
Marquis de Léon's tyranny over regions
Magic-user persecution or restriction (chapter story setup)
Infiltration of settlements by monsters or evil forces
Children being converted or corrupted by dark magic
environmental_storytelling: Decrepit towns affected by monster raids
Castles and fortifications defending against invasion
Isolated villages showing degrees of monster contact or fear
Day/night shifts in town ambiance reflecting danger cycles
